title: Find the Start and End Number of Continuous Ranges
description: Given sorted log_ids, find the start and end of each continuous range.
constraints: log_id is the primary key — all values are unique positive integers
log_id values are not necessarily consecutive — gaps indicate range boundaries
key_concept: Gaps-and-islands: log_id - ROW_NUMBER() gives a constant for consecutive sequences. Group by this key.

hints.L1: Consecutive integers form islands. Subtract ROW_NUMBER from value to get a group identifier.
hints.L2: grouping_key = log_id - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY log_id). Same island = same group key.
hints.L3: WITH t AS (SELECT log_id, log_id - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY log_id) AS grp FROM Logs) SELECT MIN(log_id) AS start_id, MAX(log_id) AS end_id FROM t GROUP BY grp ORDER BY start_id

optimal_solution:
WITH t AS (SELECT log_id, log_id - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY log_id) AS grp FROM Logs)
SELECT MIN(log_id) AS start_id, MAX(log_id) AS end_id FROM t GROUP BY grp ORDER BY start_id;
